summary.scholar: On December 14, 2012, a gunman killed 20 first-grade children and 6 staff members at Sandy Hook Elementary School in Newtown, Connecticut. The massacre, one of the deadliest school shootings in American history, reignited the national debate over gun control. Despite overwhelming public support for stricter gun laws, Congress failed to pass significant legislation in the aftermath.
summary.explorer: On December 14, 2012, a terrible shooting happened at Sandy Hook Elementary School in Connecticut. Twenty little kids and six teachers and staff were killed. It was heartbreaking. Many people called for new laws to help prevent gun violence, but it remained a very difficult issue for the country to agree on.

significance.scholar: Sandy Hook intensified the American gun control debate and became a symbol of the nation's inability to address gun violence through legislation.
significance.explorer: This terrible event made the whole country incredibly sad and started a big conversation about how to keep kids safe at school.

sources.citation: Miller Center: U.S. Presidents and Key Events
